api_version: v1 (v2 endpoints moving out of beta in 2026)

mechanisms.description: Personal API token for single-account integrations
mechanisms.notes: Simpler but less secure. Token-based rate limits (TBRL) apply from 2025. Higher burst limits for OAuth.

mechanisms.description: Required for Pipedrive Marketplace apps and commercial integrations
mechanisms.notes: OAuth apps get higher burst rate limits (e.g., 400/2s vs 100/2s for API tokens on Premium). Required for marketplace apps.

authentication.auth_header: For OAuth: Authorization: Bearer {access_token}; For API token: query param api_token={token}
authentication.v2_oauth_endpoints: New Pipedrive OAuth 2.0 flow updated for v2 endpoints

rate_limits.type: Token-Based Rate Limiting (TBRL) — rolled out fully by Dec 2025
rate_limits.daily_budget: Company-level daily API token budget, shared across all users
rate_limits.penalty: After repeated 429s, Cloudflare returns HTTP 403 with HTML error page ('blocked')
response_headers: X-RateLimit-Reset: seconds until reset (set to 10s penalty when rate limit hit)
rate_limits.retry_after_header: Set to 10 seconds (penalty window) when rate limit exceeded
rate_limits.notes: When rate limit is hit, x-ratelimit-reset is set to 10 (penalty). Even after waiting, if penalty mode is active, need to wait full penalty duration. Implement exponential backoff with jitter (start 2s, then 4s, then 8s +-500ms).

core_endpoints.description: List activities (deprecated in v1, use v2)
List deals (v2, cursor-based pagination, 50% fewer tokens)

webhooks.notes: Webhooks recommended to reduce API polling and avoid rate limits

error_dictionary.description: Request not understood — malformed JSON or invalid parameters
error_dictionary.solution: Check JSON syntax, required field presence, and parameter types

error_dictionary.description: Invalid API token or OAuth access token
error_dictionary.solution: Verify api_token query parameter or OAuth Bearer token. For OAuth, check access token hasn't expired

error_dictionary.description: Company account not open (trial expired, payment not entered)
error_dictionary.solution: Verify subscription status in Pipedrive account settings

error_dictionary.description: Request not allowed — user reached entity limit or Cloudflare block after rate limit abuse
causes: Account sent repeated 429 requests, triggering Cloudflare HTML block
error_dictionary.real_response: HTML error page: 'This error is produced by Cloudflare. See troubleshooting guide here.'
error_dictionary.solution: For rate limit blocks: wait penalty period, fix misconfiguration causing excessive requests. For entity limits: upgrade plan or archive unused records
Verify resource ID, endpoint path, and API version

error_dictionary.description: Old resource permanently unavailable (deprecated v1 endpoints)
error_dictionary.notes: Several v1 endpoints deprecated as of April 2025, to be removed after Dec 31, 2025
error_dictionary.solution: Migrate to v2 equivalent endpoints. Check changelog for deprecated endpoint list

error_dictionary.description: Feature is not enabled for the account
error_dictionary.solution: Check Pipedrive subscription plan for feature availability
Delete unused webhooks before creating new ones

Wait for Retry-After seconds (typically 10s penalty). Implement exponential backoff with jitter (2s -> 4s -> 8s + random). Use webhooks instead of polling. Migrate to OAuth for higher burst limits. Use v2 endpoints (50% fewer tokens). Use cursor-based pagination for efficiency
Retry with exponential backoff. If persistent, contact Pipedrive support
Check Pipedrive status page. Retry after maintenance window

community_constraints.issue: Rate limit penalty (10s reset) confusing — retry-after doesn't match 2s burst window
community_constraints.detail: When rate limit is hit, x-ratelimit-reset returns 10s (penalty), not the 2s burst window. Even waiting 10s may not clear the penalty without proper handling
community_constraints.workaround: Always use exponential backoff with jitter. Don't rely solely on Retry-After. Start with 2s delay, then 4s, then 8s +-500ms. Avoid thundering herd with jitter

community_constraints.issue: Multiple concurrent workers hitting rate limits independently
community_constraints.detail: Multiple background workers or webhook handlers making API calls don't know about each other's rate limit consumption, causing collective 429s
community_constraints.workaround: Use a distributed rate limit cache (Redis). Or use different API keys for different workers. Or serialize API calls through a single queue

community_constraints.issue: API v1 endpoint deprecation — many still using deprecated endpoints
community_constraints.detail: Activities, persons, products, organizations v1 endpoints deprecated April 2025, guaranteed until Dec 31, 2025 only. Must migrate to v2
community_constraints.workaround: Migrate to v2 endpoints NOW (2026). v2 uses cursor instead of offset pagination, consumes 50% fewer tokens. Update all integration code

community_constraints.issue: Custom field mapping complexity in integrations
community_constraints.detail: Pipedrive allows custom fields per account. When integrating, field keys are numeric (e.g., custom_fields.12345) and differ across accounts
community_constraints.workaround: Use API to discover custom fields dynamically. Map field keys by field name (not ID). Store field mappings per account

use_cases: Sync deals/persons between Pipedrive and external CRM or ERP
Automate activity logging from external platforms (calls, emails, meetings)
Push product data from e-commerce platforms into Pipedrive deals
Bi-directional contact sync with email marketing platforms
Create organizations and persons from web form submissions
